steps.title: Find the y-intercept by substituting x = 0
steps.content:
The y-intercept occurs on the y-axis, where $x = 0$. Substitute into $f(x)$:

$$f(0) = (-7)(4)^0 + 31$$

Since any nonzero number raised to the power of 0 equals 1, we have $(4)^0 = 1$:

$$f(0) = (-7)(1) + 31 = -7 + 31 = 24$$

The y-intercept is the point $(0, 24)$. That matches choice A — A is correct.
